parameters.bundle_policy: cluster bundles are unpromoted candidate replacements until route geometry, p75/p90, access, cue, and recertification gates pass
parameters.access_corridor_policy: start/return cue signatures identify repeated paid access corridors for same-day bundle or post-completion shrink investigation
parameters.dominance_policy: hard dominance requires known on-foot/p75/p90 no worse; lower-bound bundle dominance is only an investigation target

proves: which clusters diverge most from captured common-route templates
which route-card groups should be priced as bundle replacements instead of one-card swaps
which access and return corridors are paid by multiple current cards
which current or simulated-progress relationships can remove or shrink stale cards
does_not_prove: a route can be deleted before validated completion or recertification
lower-bound savings become real p75/on-foot savings
